The RIGHT key moved the blue blocks on the right side of the bar to the right. The grid didn't change. The bar now shows:
- Rows 19-20: `ggSSbb` (was `SSSSgg`)
- Rows 21-22: `ggSSbb` (was `bbbbgg`)
So the blue blocks moved right. The grid stayed the same. Let me try moving the blocks back to the left with LEFT, and also try UP and DOWN to see what they do.
Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The bar seems to have two independent handles:
1. Left handle (rows 17-22, cols 10-16): blue block that stays fixed
2. Right handle (rows 19-22, cols ~37-44): blue/sky blue blocks that move with LEFT/RIGHT
Maybe the right handle represents a "cursor" or "selection" on the bar. The bar might represent the 9 grid cells in some order, and the cursor selects which cell to toggle.
Let me try a different approach. Let me explore the bar more systematically to understand its range of motion and how it relates to the grid.
